rulings.Case Title: MELINDA DESIREE HARRIS VS. GRESHA MONAE BROWN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2025 Line 1. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. AMENDED The matter is continued to April 1, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 302. Plaintiff shall provide the Court with courtesty copies of all Opposition papers no later than March 7, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE ASHWORTH VS. GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE, HIGHWAY, AND TRANSPORTATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2025 Line 9. PLAINTIFF CATHERINE ASHWORTH's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. 1. Plaintiff's motion to quash/modify the subpoenas is granted. (CCP sections 1985.3(g), 1987.1.) 2. The Court limits the subpoenas to the injuries at issue in this case: brain injury, subarachnoid hemorrhage, left humerus fracture, left clavicle fracture, neck and back pain, laceration on left side of forehead, severe bruising along left side of body from shoulder to knee, and emotional distress. 3. However, at the present time, medical providers shall produce any and all records, reports, etc. from August 30, 2018, to the present. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. ECOLAB USA INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Approval And Entry Of Consent Judgments As To Ecolab Usa, Inc. And Conopco, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Tuesday, March 04, 2025 Line 5. PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH's Motion For Court Approval And Entry Of Consent Judgments As To Ecolab Usa, Inc. And Conopco, Inc. Court finds as follows: 1. Warnings comply with Prop. 65 warning requirements. 2. Based on the applicable law, attorneys' fees are reasonable. 3. The civil penalties are also reasonable based on the criteria listed in H &S Code SS 25249.7(b)(2) and 25249.7(f)(4). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
